stem: A rectangular garden has a length that is 12 times its width. The function y = 12w·w represents this situation, where y is the area, in square feet, of the garden and y > 0. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 12w in this context?

answerOptions.content: The width of the garden, in feet
The length of the garden, in feet
The area of the garden, in square feet
The difference between the length and width of the garden, in feet

rationale: Choice B is correct. The area of a rectangle is length times width. Here the area is given as 12w·w and the width is w, so the length must be 12w. Choice A reverses length and width. Choice C describes y, not 12w. Choice D would be 12w - w = 11w, not 12w.
